data.title: Chimera Investment Q1 earnings improve as book value grows 7.4%
data.description:
Chimera Investment (NYSE:CIM) stock gained 3.7% in Thursday premarket trading after the hybrid mortgage REIT's book value climbed more than analysts expected in Q1 and earnings came in slightly higher than consensus.

Q1 EPS available for distribution of $0.41, topping the average analyst estimate of $0.40, rose from $0.37 in Q4 2024 and $0.37 in last year's Q1.

Besides the improved earnings, "we also made several impactful moves on our balance sheet," said President and CEO Phillip Kardis II. "We successfully accomplished a cash-out refinancing of all the company’s outstanding non-Remic securitizations, which provided approximately $187M in funds for new investment. We also extended two large non-mark to market secured financing facilities until 2027."

GAAP book value of $21.17 per common share at March 31, 2025, climbed from $19.72 at Dec. 31, 2024, beating the consensus estimate of $19.83.

Q1 economic net interest income of $72.3M increased from $69.2M in the prior quarter and $68.0M a year ago.

Q1 economic return improved to 9.2%, compared with 4.40% for full-year 2024.

The REIT increased its provision for credit losses by $3.39M, compared with a $1.35M increase in the year-ago quarter.

Net gains on derivatives were -$2.25M vs. $10.7M a year ago.

The company recorded $8.94M in investment management and advisory fees during the quarter, compared with $0 a year earlier.

Expenses other than interest rose to $34.1M from $22.7M a year ago.

Total assets of $13.2B at March 31, 2025, compared with $13.1B at Dec. 31, 2024.

Earlier, Chimera Investment non-GAAP EPS of $0.41 beats by $0.01, revenue of $69.22M misses by $4.53M

data.title: Whitestone REIT acquires San Clemente in Texas
data.description:
• Whitestone REIT (NYSE:WSR) said it has acquired the 31,832-square-foot, restaurant-anchored San Clemente at Davenport in Texas.
  • Whitestone expects to capitalize on the center’s very strong dynamics and its own retailer relationships to position the property for long-term growth and relevance within the upscale neighborhoods it serves, the company said.

data.title: Viatris hits main goals in Phase 3 trial for birth control patch
data.description:
• Viatris (NASDAQ:VTRS) announced Thursday that Xulane Lo, a birth control patch designed to deliver a low estrogen dose for women of childbearing potential, reached primary and all secondary endpoints.
  • The Pfizer (PFE) spinoff noted that Xulane Lo indicated contraceptive efficacy as its Pearl Index, a standard used in clinical trials to calculate the efficacy of contraceptives, reached 4.14.
  • Additionally, the product was found to have a favorable safety and tolerability profile, with most treatment-emergent adverse events being mild to moderate in severity.
  • The trial evaluated the Xulane Lo low-dose weekly dermal patch in over 1,200 healthy women aged at least 16 years in 81 study sites across the U.S., Puerto Rico, and Canada.
  • The company plans to submit a new drug application seeking U.S. FDA approval for Xulane Lo in the second half of 2025.
